  name: ingress-nginx-controller
  namespace: ingress-nginx
  resourceVersion: "804401"
  uid: db703acf-6e39-4d96-a7d5-1646fb26a147
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/component: controller
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: ingress-nginx
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 1
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/component: controller
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/part-of: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/version: 1.14.1
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: true
      containers:
      - args:
        - /nginx-ingress-controller
        - --election-id=ingress-nginx-leader
        - --controller-class=k8s.io/ingress-nginx
        - --ingress-class=nginx
        - --configmap=$(POD_NAMESPACE)/ingress-nginx-controller
        - --validating-webhook=:8443
        - --validating-webhook-certificate=/usr/local/certificates/cert
        - --validating-webhook-key=/usr/local/certificates/key
        - --watch-ingress-without-class=true
        - --publish-status-address=localhost
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: POD_NAMESPACE
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace
        - name: LD_PRELOAD
          value: /usr/local/lib/libmimalloc.so
        image: registry.k8s.io/ingress-nginx/controller:v1.14.1@sha256:f95a79b85fb93ac3de752c71a5c27d5ceae10a18b61904dec224c1c6a4581e47
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        lifecycle:
          preStop:
            exec:
              command:
              - /wait-shutdown
        livenessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 5
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 10254
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        name: controller
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          hostPort: 80
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 443
          hostPort: 443
          name: https
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8443
          name: webhook
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:

content.thinking:
The configmap is currently empty (no data). I need to add the OpenTelemetry configuration. Let me check what version of ingress-nginx is running and what OTel options are supported.

The controller is v1.14.1. The OpenTelemetry support in ingress-nginx was added in v1.8.0, so it's supported.

The configuration I need to add to the configmap:
1. `enable-opentelemetry: "true"` - to enable OTel
2. `opentelemetry-collector-host: "otel-collector.otel.svc"` - the OTel collector host
3. `opentelemetry-collector-port: "4317"` - the OTel collector port (gRPC)
4. `otel-sampler: "AlwaysOn"` - to enable 100% sampling
5. `log-format-upstream` - I'll need to configure a custom log format that includes trace and span IDs

Now I need to verify the exact configmap keys supported in v1.14.1 to make sure I'm using the right parameter names. Let me verify the exact ConfigMap keys for ingress-nginx v1.14.1, since the OpenTelemetry support and naming conventions may have evolved across versions. When OTel is enabled in ingress-nginx, I get access to `$opentelemetry_trace_id` and `$opentelemetry_span_id` variables that I can use in the log format. My approach is to patch the controller's ConfigMap with the necessary OTel settings—enabling it, pointing to the collector endpoint, configuring the sampler to always capture traces, and updating the log format to include those trace variables—then wait for the controller to pick up the changes.

content.text: The configmap is currently empty. I'll now patch it to enable OpenTelemetry with the required settings and custom log format.

input.description: Patch ingress-nginx configmap to enable OTel and set log format

content.thinking: Good. Now I need to wait for the ingress-nginx controller to pick up the config changes. The controller watches the configmap and should reload nginx automatically. Let me wait a bit and then check the controller logs to see if it's reloaded.

content.thinking: The controller has reloaded the configuration. I can see the "Backend successfully reloaded" message. Now let me send the test request and check the logs to see if the OTel trace/span IDs are appearing.

content.text: The controller picked up the ConfigMap change and reloaded. Now let me send the test request and check the logs.

input.command: kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: otel.example.com" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/otel-check 2>&1
input.description: Send test request to generate an access log entry
